content.text: SLSA Provenance for Build Pipelines

SLSA (Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts) is a security framework that defines what claims a build system can make about how an artifact was produced. A SLSA provenance document is a signed statement answering: what source commit was built, on what build platform, with what commands, and what artifact was produced. For GenAI services, SLSA provenance closes a critical gap — it makes the path from source code to deployed container image auditable and tamper-evident.

A leaked model weight, a compromised dependency, or a malicious CI plugin can silently alter what gets deployed. SLSA provenance does not prevent these attacks but makes them detectable: if the deployed image's provenance does not match what your CI pipeline signed, admission control can block the deployment.

attrs.alt: SLSA levels and build attestation chain from source to Kubernetes deployment

content.text: What SLSA Provenance Contains
A SLSA provenance document follows the in-toto attestation format with a SLSA-specific predicate. The key fields establish an unbroken chain from source commit to output artifact.
Generating SLSA Provenance with slsa-github-generator

The SLSA GitHub Generator project provides reusable workflows that produce SLSA L3 provenance automatically. The provenance is generated in an isolated, ephemeral job that cannot be influenced by the build job's code.

# .github/workflows/build.yml
name: Build and Attest

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Build and push container image
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          push: true
          tags: registry.internal/genai-service:${{ github.sha }}
          # digest output: sha256:a3f2c8...

  # SLSA provenance generation runs in a separate, isolated job
  provenance:
    needs: build
    permissions:
      actions: read       # required to read workflow run
      id-token: write     # required for keyless signing
      packages: write     # required to push attestation to registry
    uses: slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator/.github/workflows/generator_container_slsa3.yml@v1.10.0
    with:
      image: registry.internal/genai-service
      digest: ${{ needs.build.outputs.image-digest }}
      registry-username: ${{ github.actor }}
    secrets:
      registry-password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

The slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator workflow is pinned to a specific SHA in production — never use a floating tag like @main for security-critical workflows. The SHA ensures the provenance generator itself cannot be silently swapped.
Verifying Provenance at Deploy Time

Generating provenance is worthless without verification. Add a cosign verify step in the deployment pipeline that confirms the image was built from your repository by your CI system before it is allowed into the cluster.

#!/bin/bash
# scripts/verify-provenance.sh
# Verifies SLSA provenance before deployment.
# Fails if the image was not built by the expected CI identity.

set -euo pipefail

IMAGE_REF="${1}"   # e.g. registry.internal/genai-service@sha256:a3f2c8...
GITHUB_ORG="org"
REPO="genai-service"

echo "Verifying SLSA provenance for: $IMAGE_REF"

# Verify the image signature
cosign verify \
    --certificate-identity-regexp "https://github.com/${GITHUB_ORG}/${REPO}/.*" \
    --certificate-oidc-issuer "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
    "${IMAGE_REF}"

# Verify and extract SLSA provenance attestation
cosign verify-attestation \
    --type slsaprovenance \
    --certificate-identity-regexp "https://github.com/slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator/.*" \
    --certificate-oidc-issuer "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
    "${IMAGE_REF}" \
    | jq -r '.payload | @base64d | fromjson' > provenance.json

# Check the source repository matches what we expect
SOURCE_URI=$(jq -r '.predicate.materials[0].uri' provenance.json)
EXPECTED_URI="git+https://github.com/${GITHUB_ORG}/${REPO}@refs/heads/main"

if [[ "$SOURCE_URI" != "$EXPECTED_URI" ]]; then
    echo "FAIL: unexpected source URI: $SOURCE_URI"
    echo "Expected: $EXPECTED_URI"
    exit 1
fi

echo "PASS: SLSA provenance verified. Built from: $SOURCE_URI"
Admission Control: Enforce Provenance in Kubernetes

Script-based verification at deploy time can be bypassed if someone deploys directly with kubectl. Enforce provenance verification at the admission controller level using Kyverno or OPA/Gatekeeper so no unsigned or improperly-attested image can run in the cluster.

# k8s/policy/require-slsa-provenance.yaml (Kyverno ClusterPolicy)
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-slsa-provenance
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Enforce
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: check-slsa-attestation
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds: [Pod]
              namespaces: [genai-prod, genai-staging]
      verifyImages:
        - imageReferences:
            - "registry.internal/genai-service:*"
          attestations:
            - predicateType: "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v0.2"
              conditions:
                - all:
                    - key: "{{ predicate.builder.id }}"
                      operator: Equals
                      value: "https://github.com/actions/runner"
                    - key: "{{ predicate.invocation.configSource.uri }}"
                      operator: Equals
                      value: "git+https://github.com/org/genai-service@refs/heads/main"
          attestors:
            - count: 1
              entries:
                - keyless:
                    issuer: "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com"
                    subject: "https://github.com/slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator/.github/workflows/generator_container_slsa3.yml@*"
SLSA for GenAI: Special Considerations

GenAI services have build-time artifacts beyond the application container that also warrant provenance: fine-tuned model weights, evaluation datasets, and prompt template files. While SLSA is designed for software artifacts, the same attestation approach applies.
Artifact
Provenance Approach
Signing Tool
Verification Point
Container image
SLSA provenance via slsa-github-generator
cosign (keyless)
Kubernetes admission controller
Fine-tuned weights
Custom in-toto attestation with training config hash
cosign blob sign
Model-loading code + deploy gate
Eval dataset
SHA-256 manifest signed at dataset creation
cosign blob sign
Eval pipeline at run start
Prompt templates
Include in container image (covered by image provenance)
cosign (via image)
Image provenance verification

# Sign a model weights file with cosign (keyless)
# Run in the fine-tuning pipeline after training completes

MODEL_PATH="models/genai-finetuned-v3.safetensors"
MODEL_DIGEST=$(sha256sum "$MODEL_PATH" | cut -d' ' -f1)

# Sign the model file
cosign sign-blob \
    --bundle model-v3.bundle \
    "$MODEL_PATH"

# Record training provenance alongside the model
cat > model-v3-provenance.json << EOF
{
  "model_id": "genai-finetuned-v3",
  "base_model": "meta-llama/Llama-3-8B",
  "training_run": "$TRAINING_RUN_ID",
  "dataset_sha256": "$DATASET_DIGEST",
  "training_config_sha256": "$(sha256sum training_config.yaml | cut -d' ' -f1)",
  "built_by": "$GITHUB_ACTOR",
  "built_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "artifact_sha256": "$MODEL_DIGEST"
}
EOF

echo "Model provenance recorded: model-v3-provenance.json"
echo "Digest: $MODEL_DIGEST"
Provenance Verification in CI Before Promotion

Provenance verification should not only happen at Kubernetes admission time — it should also gate environment promotion. Before promoting a build from staging to production, a verification step confirms the image's provenance is valid and its source commit was from the expected branch with the required number of approvals.

# scripts/verify_promotion_gate.py
# Called by the staging → prod promotion pipeline.
# Fails if the image lacks valid SLSA provenance or was built from an unexpected source.

import subprocess, json, sys, os

EXPECTED_REPO = "git+https://github.com/org/genai-service@refs/heads/main"
EXPECTED_BUILDER = "https://github.com/actions/runner"
COSIGN_ISSUER = "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com"
COSIGN_ID_REGEX = "https://github.com/slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator/.*"

def verify_slsa_provenance(image_ref: str) -> dict:
    result = subprocess.run([
        "cosign", "verify-attestation",
        "--type", "slsaprovenance",
        "--certificate-identity-regexp", COSIGN_ID_REGEX,
        "--certificate-oidc-issuer", COSIGN_ISSUER,
        image_ref,
    ], capture_output=True, text=True)

    if result.returncode != 0:
        raise ValueError(f"Provenance verification failed:\n{result.stderr}")

    # Parse the first attestation
    lines = [l for l in result.stdout.splitlines() if l.strip()]
    attestation = json.loads(lines[0])
    import base64
    payload = json.loads(base64.b64decode(attestation["payload"]))
    return payload["predicate"]

def check_promotion_gate(image_ref: str) -> None:
    print(f"Verifying SLSA provenance for promotion: {image_ref}")
    predicate = verify_slsa_provenance(image_ref)

    builder_id = predicate.get("builder", {}).get("id", "")
    source_uri = predicate.get("materials", [{}])[0].get("uri", "")

    if builder_id != EXPECTED_BUILDER:
        raise SystemExit(f"GATE FAIL: unexpected builder: {builder_id}")

    if source_uri != EXPECTED_REPO:
        raise SystemExit(f"GATE FAIL: unexpected source: {source_uri}")

    print(f"GATE PASS: built by {builder_id}")
    print(f"GATE PASS: from source {source_uri}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    check_promotion_gate(sys.argv[1])
Hardening the Build for SLSA L3

SLSA L3 adds three requirements beyond L2: the build environment must be isolated (no network access to external registries during build), build parameters must not be user-controllable at run time (all inputs are in version-controlled files), and the provenance must record the exact versions of all build tools.

# .github/workflows/build-hardened.yml
# SLSA L3 hardened build: isolated, parameterized, fully recorded.

jobs:
  build-hardened:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Restrict job permissions to minimum required
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # Pin to exact SHA — prevents tag-redirect attacks
          ref: ${{ github.sha }}

      # Record all tool versions in the provenance
      - name: Record build environment
        run: |
          echo "DOCKER_VERSION=$(docker --version)" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "BUILDKIT_VERSION=$(docker buildx version)" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "PYTHON_VERSION=$(python3 --version)" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      # Build with BuildKit for reproducible layer ordering
      - name: Build image (no network during build)
        run: |
          docker buildx build \
            --network=none \        # L3: no outbound network during build
            --no-cache \            # ensures reproducibility
            --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=0 \
            --output type=image,name=registry.internal/genai-service,push=true \
            --metadata-file build-metadata.json \
            .

      - name: Extract image digest
        id: digest
        run: echo "digest=$(jq -r '."containerimage.digest"' build-metadata.json)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

--network=none during docker build prevents dependencies from being fetched at build time from unexpected sources. All packages must be in the requirements.txt lock file and cached in the build context — a useful validation that your dependency pins are complete.
Summary
SLSA provenance is a signed attestation answering: what source commit was built, by what CI system, to produce what artifact — it makes the source-to-container chain auditable.
Target SLSA L2 (signed provenance from hosted CI) as the minimum for GenAI production services; regulated deployments require L3 (isolated build, no secret injection).
Use slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator reusable workflows — the provenance is generated in an isolated job that cannot be influenced by build code.
Verify provenance at deploy time with cosign verify-attestation, checking both the signing identity and the source repository URI.
Enforce provenance at the admission controller level (Kyverno or OPA/Gatekeeper) so no image bypasses verification even with direct kubectl access.
Extend the attestation model beyond containers: sign model weights, eval datasets, and training configs with cosign blob sign and record provenance alongside each artifact.
